title: Evaluate BLE IPSP versus NUS SLIP for T-Deck IP local link
description: Evaluate whether the LilyGO T-Deck Zephyr firmware should expose IP/CoAP local-client traffic over standards-based BLE IPSP/6LoWPAN, BLE NUS carrying SLIP-framed IPv6 as a practical equivalent, or both. Current T-Deck config keeps NUS/SLIP disabled because BLE egress is not wired and the board must not advertise an incomplete local-client service. Acceptance: decision recorded with Zephyr ESP32-S3 support evidence, native app impact, UUID/advertising behavior, and follow-up implementation Beads for any selected unsupported transport.
notes: No-hardware decision evidence (2026-07-02): recorded T-Deck BLE IP transport decision in docs/ble-app-surface-owner.md. Decision: T-Deck native local-client MVP uses LICHEN-specific native BLE LCI with SLIP-framed IPv6 over GATT; do not select BLE IPSP/6LoWPAN for T-Deck MVP and do not advertise legacy NUS as the default T-Deck native surface. Evidence: Zephyr latest docs list ESP32-S3 Bluetooth LE and ESP32 Bluetooth HCI support, but the documented IPSP sample is shown for nrf52840dk/nrf52840. Local pinned Zephyr v3.7.0 has residual IPSS UUID/link-address definitions, but release notes state IPSP support was removed and CONFIG_NET_L2_BT no longer exists; local tree also lacks samples/bluetooth/ipsp. Existing LICHEN firmware contract already defines native BLE LCI as SLIP-framed IPv6 over LICHEN-specific GATT UUIDs with version/capabilities. T-Deck board config keeps CONFIG_LICHEN_HAS_BLE_LOCAL=n and CONFIG_SLIP=n so it does not advertise an incomplete service. Product T-Deck BLE-local enablement remains gated on no-hardware tests plus physical T-Deck validation evidence. Follow-up created: project-LICHEN-h7t5.7.6.1.1 to re-evaluate IPSP only if Zephyr ESP32-S3 build/runtime evidence and restored/replaced IPSP network L2 support are available. Existing related follow-ups cover native client UUID discovery (project-LICHEN-h7t5.7.6.2.3), BLE ResourceTransport bridge (project-LICHEN-q279), and physical T-Deck validation (project-LICHEN-h7t5.7.6.2.4). Checks: git diff --check PASS. Reviews: Hubble repo exploration found no local IPSP implementation and confirmed existing native BLE LCI/T-Deck-disabled policy; Maxwell review no P0/P1/P2 with P3 last-touched metadata note only; Locke initially found P2 enablement gate and pinned-Zephyr ambiguity, both fixed, re-review clean; Hume initially found P2 that Zephyr 3.7 removed IPSP/CONFIG_NET_L2_BT, fixed in doc and follow-up Bead, re-review no P0/P1/P2.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware transport decision: T-Deck native local-client MVP uses LICHEN-specific BLE LCI with SLIP-framed IPv6 over GATT; BLE IPSP is deferred because pinned Zephyr v3.7.0 removed IPSP/CONFIG_NET_L2_BT and latest Zephyr IPSP docs target nRF52840dk, not T-Deck/ESP32-S3. Follow-up project-LICHEN-h7t5.7.6.1.1 tracks future IPSP reevaluation; existing Beads track client UUID discovery, BLE ResourceTransport, and physical validation.
